title: Cozy Slow Cooker Beef Stew That Melts Hearts
promptIMG: Food photography: beef stew, appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2
description: This slow cooker beef stew is pure comfort in a bowl. With tender meat and hearty veggies, it's perfect for chilly evenings. Just set it and forget it!
ingredients: 2 pounds beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Transfer the browned beef to the slow cooker. Add the chopped onion, garlic, carrots, and potatoes.
In a bowl, mix the beef broth, tomato paste, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Pour this mixture over the beef and vegetables.
Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4 hours, until the beef is tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary before serving.
tips: One key tip is to sear the beef before adding it to the slow cooker; it locks in flavor and gives a nice texture. A common mistake is overcooking the stew; check for doneness after the recommended time. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days, and they taste even better the next day!

structured_content.content: There's something undeniably comforting about a bowl of beef stew, especially when it's made in a slow cooker. This cozy slow cooker beef stew is my go-to recipe for chilly evenings when all I want is something hearty and warm. The best part? You can set it in the morning and come home to a deliciously fragrant house!

This stew is all about quality ingredients. I love using beef chuck because it becomes so tender after hours of slow cooking. If you’re looking for a lighter option, you can swap in chicken or even go vegetarian with some hearty mushrooms and lentils!

The process is super easy. After browning the beef, just toss everything into the slow cooker and let it do its magic. You'll want to check the seasoning before serving; sometimes a little extra salt or pepper can really elevate the flavors.

upload.context: Bowl of beef stew on a wooden table with rustic background

structured_content.content: This beef stew is perfect for family gatherings or a cozy night in. Serve it with a crusty piece of bread to soak up all that rich broth, or over a bed of creamy mashed potatoes for an extra indulgent touch. If you want to mix things up, try adding some green beans or peas in the last hour of cooking for a pop of color and flavor.
